description: Figure 3 presents CALPHAD-calculated austenite (FCC) phase fraction as a function of temperature for an 8Mn-0.2C (wt.%) low-density steel alloy containing either 6 wt.% Al or 8 wt.% Al, using three thermodynamic databases: PrecHiMn-01 (light gray solid), PrecHiMn-02 (black dashed), and PrecHiMn-03 (gray solid). For each alloy composition, the curves rise from near-zero fraction at approximately 600 °C to a maximum between roughly 900 and 1000 °C before declining toward zero near 1400 °C. The 8 wt.% Al variant consistently exhibits higher predicted austenite stability than the 6 wt.% Al variant across all databases. Notably, the PrecHiMn-02 database predicts substantially higher peak austenite fractions, reaching about 0.75 for 8% Al, compared with PrecHiMn-01 and PrecHiMn-03 which peak around 0.43–0.48. The first and third database versions yield similar curve shapes but are shifted in temperature. The caption and citation note persistent discrepancies among databases regarding the fcc + bcc + κ three-phase domain and emphasize that experimental validation is still needed, underscoring that accurate thermodynamic descriptions are critical for alloy composition and continuous annealing process design in these lightweight steels.
